source: ACCJC - Accrediting Commission for Community and Junior Colleges

standards.title: Mission, Academic Quality and Institutional Effectiveness
standards.description: The institution demonstrates strong commitment to a clearly stated mission that guides institutional planning and operations. It has measurable student learning outcomes and evaluates its effectiveness in achieving them.

substandards.title: Mission
substandards.description: The institution has a clearly defined, widely published mission that guides its operations.

substandards.title: Assuring Academic Quality and Institutional Effectiveness
substandards.description: The institution assures the quality of its educational offerings through its evaluation of how well student learning is being achieved.

substandards.title: Institutional Planning and Effectiveness
substandards.description: The institution conducts planning and institutional effectiveness evaluation of relevant, accessible, and reliable information.

keyQuestions: Does the program review address the institution's mission?
Are learning outcomes clearly defined and assessed?
Are the effectiveness indicators tracked and analyzed?
How does the program contribute to institutional goals?

standards.title: Student Learning and Support Services
standards.description: The institution provides learning opportunities and student support services appropriate to its mission. Institutions ensure that students are prepared to succeed in a complex society and economy.

substandards.title: Instructional Programs
substandards.description: The institution offers instructional programs, including general education, in formats and locations that accommodate student diversity and needs.

substandards.title: Library and Learning Support Services
substandards.description: The institution provides library and learning support services to support student needs and learning.

substandards.title: Student Support Services
substandards.description: The institution provides appropriate, comprehensive, and accessible student support services.

keyQuestions: Are student learning outcomes being assessed?
What support services are available to students?
How are assessment results used to improve programs?
Are there adequate resources for student success?

standards.title: Resources
standards.description: The institution effectively uses all of its resources to achieve its mission and to ensure the quality, stability, and integrity of its educational programs and services.

substandards.title: Human Resources
substandards.description: The institution employs a sufficient number of qualified personnel to carry out its mission.

substandards.title: Physical Resources and Facilities
substandards.description: The institution has appropriate physical resources and facilities to support its mission and ensure the safety and well-being of its students.

substandards.title: Technology Resources and Support
substandards.description: The institution ensures adequate technology resources and support for teaching, learning, and institutional operations.

substandards.title: Financial Resources
substandards.description: The institution allocates and manages its financial resources to support student learning programs and services and to improve institutional effectiveness.

keyQuestions: Does the program have sufficient staff and resources?
Are facilities adequate for the program's needs?
Is the budget allocation aligned with program goals?
What RAR (Resource Allocation Request) needs exist?

standards.title: Leadership and Governance
standards.description: The institution demonstrates effective overall planning, leadership, and governance structures that promote student learning and accomplishment of mission through collaborative processes that involve the broad college community.

substandards.title: Decision-Making Roles and Processes
substandards.description: The institution has a clear system of governance with defined roles and responsibilities that supports effective decision-making and planning.

substandards.title: Chief Executive Officer
substandards.description: The institution's chief executive officer provides effective leadership in planning, governance, and institutional improvement.

substandards.title: Governing Board
substandards.description: The governing board acts in the best interests of the institution in setting policies and ensuring effective governance.

keyQuestions: Are program decisions aligned with institutional governance?
Does the program participate in collaborative planning?
Are program goals aligned with institutional goals?
How does leadership support program improvement?

standard_I.title: Mission & Institutional Effectiveness
items: Clear mission statement aligned with program description
Evidence of closing the loop (using assessment data for improvement)

standard_II.title: Student Learning & Support
items: Student learning outcomes are specific and measurable
Assessment plan is comprehensive and ongoing
Student support services identified and evaluated
Evidence of student success in program completion
Equity data disaggregated by demographic groups
Gap analysis and improvement plans documented

standard_III.title: Resources
items: Budget allocated to support program goals
Technology resources are appropriate and current
Resource allocation aligned with program priorities

standard_IV.title: Leadership & Governance
items: Program aligns with college strategic plan

evaluationCriteria.description: Does the program demonstrate how it supports the college mission and student success?
Are SLOs clearly defined, measurable, and evidence-based?
Is there documented evidence of assessment and continuous improvement?
Does the program demonstrate commitment to equity in student outcomes?
Are staffing, facilities, and budget adequate for program goals?
Is the program integrated with institutional planning and governance?
How do external factors (market, regulations, competition) impact the program?
Does the program have a clear planning agenda aligned with college goals?

commonIssues.feedback: Programs must demonstrate systematic assessment of student learning outcomes with documented evidence of student mastery.
Clarify how the program contributes to the college mission and institutional strategic priorities.
Document need for staffing, facilities, or technology through data. Submit RARs (Resource Allocation Requests) to justify additional resources.
Show how assessment results led to programmatic changes and improvements in student outcomes.
Disaggregate data by student demographics. Identify and address disparities in student achievement and support.
Review program goals and objectives. Ensure they are current, measurable, and aligned with institutional strategic plan.
